substep.title: Assess Plan Count
substep.action:
Review the design decisions + vertical slices. Determine:
- Single plan (default): one set of related slices that implement the entire design in one coordinated effort - Multi-plan: design surfaces N architecturally-distinct plannable areas (NOT sub-tickets — the ticket stays one ticket; the implementation produces multiple plan docs)
Criteria for multi-plan: - The work splits along clear module boundaries - Each split can produce a coherent plan independently - The total work is large enough that splitting yields real benefit (parallel-safe execution or sequential focus)
Most implementations are single-plan. Multi-plan emerges from architecturally-large features or infrastructure work.

substep.title: Document in Design File
substep.action:
Under "### Multi-plan identification" in the design doc, write either:
- Single plan: "This design produces one plan covering all {{N}} vertical slices."
OR
- Multi-plan: "This design produces {{N}} plans: - Plan 1: {{scope; which slices}} - Plan 2: {{scope; which slices}} - ..."

substep.title: Confirm With User
